channel.title: Comments for page "Automated Passive Amnestization System Ver. 17.09"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "Automated Passive Amnestization System Ver. 17.09"

item.title: Changelong (?)
item.content:encoded:
Well, it's been a year, and I've become relatively more knowledgeable on CSS, site norms, and general storytelling. As such I've made a couple of recent changes:

  • Cut the number of pages down from 7 to 5.
  • Moved the "What is APAS?" diatribe out of the green terminal to avoid eye strain.
  • Made the hidden text in the MDP page appear on hover, instead of forcing readers to copy/paste externally.
  • Other near-unnoticable updates, like removing the terminal scanline.

I'm tempted to just do a full rewrite but for now, I've stopped myself from altering the actual text too much. Thank you all for your patience.

item.title: Re: Wow
item.content:encoded:
You can check code-related questions like this by scrolling to the bottom of the page, clicking +Options, and clicking Page Source.

This article uses the ListPages module to do the whole "multiple pages in one" thing, so you may need to open up the fragment pages that it pulls from to see whatever code you're asking for. I've listed them all in the page source.

item.title: Wow
item.content:encoded: How did you make a module like that?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I am Apollyon, and I have no equal.

AreYouSureAboutThat.mp4

Essentially what’s keeping me at a novote. There’s just too much worldending shit in the SCPverse

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks for the question; as explained in a footnote next to "essophyical":

Essophysical entities are capable of passively exerting an effect on baseline reality and are generally unobservable due to their omnipresence and non-physicality.

Basically "embodiments of concepts", but flipped around; concepts only exist because these entities exist. SCP-4260 explains this further:

Primitive cultures referred to them as gods, which is a bit of a misnomer; we call them Intrinsics. Gods are reality-bending entities, usually with their own agendas and identities: it may help to remember The Deer. Intrinsics are concepts that cannot be separated from our cosmos without us all living in a radically different world. They’re more concept than entity, and rarely take any sort of physical form.

"Esso" here denotes essence, I suppose.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I have but one question right now: Does the "esso-" in "essophysical" mean anything? The one thing that I could find is the word "essonite", where "esso-" comes from Ancient Greek ἥσσων (hḗssōn) which supposedly means "inferior"; if that's the source of "esso-" in your term, then that would make "essophysical" roughly mean "below the physical" (which I guess is a nice parallel to "metaphysical" meaning "beyond/above the physical"). Is that right?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Happy you liked it! I recently uploaded the Explained page, if you’re interested: http://scpexplained.wikidot.com/apas

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Holy shit was that a ride. I am by no means an expert in writing SCPs, but I'll be damned if this wasn't one of the most fascinating and capturing articles I've read in a while. I still don't fully understand waht's going on if I'm being honest, but you can tell this is some good writing if you still feel satisfied without really grasping it all.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Brilliant, dude. I did not expect this to go where it went, but I'm glad you did what you did with it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hey, welcome to my tale. Planning on using this storyline for my later tales. Hope you enjoyed!

Dr. Weathers is a character from my other tale, He Who Screws With Reality.

File name "dro-0001" taken from NVE's Flickr.
